title: cyclophyllum tiebaghiense
intro: Cyclophyllum tiebaghiense is a plant species native to Madagascar, belonging to the family Rubiaceae, commonly known as the coffee or madder family. The plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, particularly in its leaves and stems, which have been traditionally used to treat various ailments. The leaves of the Cyclophyllum tiebaghiense plant are said to have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which provide several health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating pain, and treating respiratory issues. The plant is often used in herbal preparations, such as teas and infusions, to harness its medicinal properties and provide relief from various health conditions.
